subject: First Year Topology
instructions: Work Problems 1–5 and show all work. Support all statements to the best of your ability. Answer Problems 6–10 with complete definitions or statements or proofs.

problems.text: Show that the torus \(T\) is not homeomorphic to the surface of genus 2, \(M_2=T\mathbin{\#}T\).
Prove that the set of irrational numbers \(\mathcal I\subset\mathbb R\) cannot be presented as a countable union of closed sets each of which has empty interior in \(\mathcal I\).
Let \(CX\) denote the cone over the \(n\)-point space \(X=\{1,\ldots,n\}\). Show that every continuous map \(f:CX\to CX\) has a fixed point.
Show that the fundamental group of \(M=K\mathbin{\#}T\) is not abelian, where \(K\) is the Klein bottle.
Show that every map \(f:S^2\to\prod_{\alpha\in J}S^1\) of the 2-sphere to the product of circles is null-homotopic.
Show that for a retraction \(r:X\to A\), the induced map \(r_\#\) on fundamental groups is surjective.
Which of the following spaces, taken with the product topology, are locally compact? separable? Here \(I=[0,1]\) and \(\mathbb N\) is the set of natural numbers.
State the Seifert–van Kampen Theorem. Can it be used to compute the fundamental group of \(S^1\)?
Show that \(S^2\) is not homeomorphic to \(S^4\).
